title: Incorrectly Flipping Bits to Zero Count

statement.markdown: Count the number of bits that need to be flipped in order to make all bits equal to the least common bit. The least common bit is the bit that appears least frequently across all bits in the input.
statement.inputFormat: A single integer n representing the number of bits.
statement.outputFormat: An integer representing the number of bits that need to be flipped.
statement.notes: The least common bit can be 0 or 1.

examples.explanation: For the input [1, 2, 3, 4], the least common bit is 1. We need to flip 2 bits to make all bits equal to 1.

hints.text: Consider using the XOR operator to find the least common bit.

editorial.title: Solving the Incorrectly Flipping Bits to Zero Count
editorial.intuition: We can count the number of bits for 0 and 1 separately and then choose the less frequent one.
editorial.bruteForce: We can iterate over each bit and count the number of times each bit appears, then choose the less frequent one.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the XOR operator to find the least common bit and then count the number of bits that are different from this bit.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This solution works because XORing a bit with the least common bit will flip the bit if it is equal to the least common bit.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int flipBits(int n) { int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) { if (((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> i) & 1)) count++; } return count; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean flipBits(int n) { int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) { if (((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> i) & 1)) count++; } return count > 0; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def flipBits(self, n: int) -> int: count = 0; for i in range(32): if ((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> i) & 1): count++; return count
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function flipBits(n) { var count = 0; for (var i = 0; i < 32; i++) { if (((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> i) & 1)) count++; } return count; }

solutions.python:
import sys class Solution: def flipBits(self, n: int) -> int: c = 0 for i in range(32): if ((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> (n.bit_length() - 1)) & 1): c += 1 return c
if __name__ == '__main__': n = int(input()) sys.stdout.write(str(Solution().flipBits(n)))
solutions.cpp:
#include <iostream> class Solution { public: int flipBits(int n) { int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) { if (((n >> i) & 1) != ((n >> (31 - __builtin_clz(n))) & 1)) count++; } return count; } }; int main() { int n; std::cin >> n; std::cout << Solution().flipBits(n) << '
'; return 0; }
